libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker service or external Jackrabbit/WebDAV server appears necessary. Prefer in-memory coverage of WebDAV methods, XML/property/report objects, and minimal HttpServletRequest/HttpServletResponse stubs or proxies for servlet-facing APIs. Use javax.servlet APIs, not jakarta.servlet.

deterministic_setup.reason:
org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4 declares servlet-api as provided, while key public classes such as WebdavRequestImpl, WebdavResponseImpl, AbstractWebdavServlet, InputContextImpl, and OutputContextImpl depend on javax.servlet/http types that are not pulled transitively into the test
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Omitting the servlet API will make tests that exercise important server-side WebDAV classes fail at compilation.
Using a modern jakarta.servlet dependency would not satisfy the library because the artifact imports javax.servlet.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jackrabbit WebDAV exposes servlet-facing request/response and WebDAV servlet APIs, but its Maven POM marks javax.servlet:servlet-api as provided, so generated tests need that API added explicitly to compile and cover meaningful server-side classes.
